response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and systems software engineer with a broad career spanning web, mobile, and low-latency services, but his background has virtually no overlap with the core requirements of this Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware-software co-design, and deep-learning workload performance analysis — areas in which the candidate shows no demonstrated experience. His closest relevant work (low-latency async I/O at Sociomantic, robotic arm firmware at Altom) is tangentially adjacent at best, and his recent trajectory has moved further from hardware into web-centric AI tooling and full-stack development.
strengths: Demonstrated low-latency systems programming experience handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and high-performance databases (Sociomantic, DLang).
Some exposure to hardware-adjacent domains including robotic arm development and a bachelor's thesis on wave field synthesis with multi-channel audio hardware.
Long (16+ year) career with demonstrated ability to work across multiple abstraction layers (embedded, systems, web, cloud), suggesting adaptability.
concerns: Zero demonstrated experience in SoC or IP modeling, chip architecture, or any silicon-level design — the primary technical requirement of the role.
No background in AI/ML workload optimization, neural network training or inference, GPU architecture, or hardware accelerator design (required and preferred qualifications).
Current and recent trajectory is firmly in web full-stack and AI-agent tooling (TypeScript, Node.js, MCP frameworks), moving away from rather than toward hardware performance engineering.

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or GPU architecture background
